content.problemStatement:
Alice and Bob are playing a modified version of the Nim game. There are NNN piles of stones, where the ithi^{th}ith pile contains AiA_iAi​ stones.

In each turn, Alice and Bob roll a fair DDD-sided die. The player who rolls the higher number gets to make a move. If they roll the same number, Alice gets to make the move.

During their turn, a player must select **exactly one** non-empty pile and remove **atleast one** stone from it. **The player who makes the last valid move wins the game.**

Given that both players play optimally, calculate the probability that Alice will win the game.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input:

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and DDD — the number of piles and the number of faces in the dice respectively.
The second lines contains NNN space-separated integers denoting the number of stones in each pile initially.




Output Format
For each test case, find the probability of Alice wining the game.


The probability can be written in the form of PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​ such that QQQ is not divisible by 109+710^9 + 7109+7. Output the value of P⋅Q−1mod  (109+7)P \cdot Q^{-1} \mod (10^9 + 7)P⋅Q−1mod(109+7).

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Probability of Alice winning is 23\dfrac{2}{3}32​.
